title: Bug fixes (4) + liquidity-adjusted position sizing in portfolio optimizer

bugs_found: covariance.py:279 — covariance_diagnostics uses marchenko_pastur_bound with default var=1.0 instead of fitted noise variance, giving inconsistent MP bound vs what denoise_covariance actually computes
tail_dependence.py:287 — BFS cluster detection uses list.pop(0) which is O(n) per pop, making the BFS O(n²) instead of O(n)
covariance.py:201 — dead variable start_idx assigned after detoning but never used in reconstruction
return_model.py:82 — targets are sliced to [:train_end_idx] on line 65, then redundantly re-sliced on line 82, creating unnecessary copy
observation.gap_identified: Portfolio optimizer produces weights without considering liquidity constraints. Illiquid positions can cause slippage, exit risk, and unreliable marking. No mechanism to penalize or cap illiquid allocations.

bugs_fixed: covariance.py diagnostics now uses _fit_mp_variance to compute noise variance, consistent with denoise_covariance
tail_dependence.py BFS now uses collections.deque with popleft() for O(1) per pop
return_model.py removed redundant double-slice of targets in _train_single loop
implementation.feature_built: Liquidity-adjusted position sizing: adjust_weights_for_liquidity() function that post-processes optimizer weights by (1) hard-flooring positions below min dollar volume threshold, (2) penalizing stocks with liquidity score below threshold using configurable exponent, (3) redistributing freed weight pro-rata to liquid positions, (4) renormalizing. Integrated into compare_methods() so all optimizer comparisons include liquidity-adjusted alternatives.

limitations: Liquidity scores require live yfinance data when not pre-provided — adds latency to compare_methods
Liquidity penalty exponent and threshold are heuristic, not calibrated to empirical slippage data
BFS fix is minor — the cluster graph is always small (≤20 nodes)
Did not add API endpoint for standalone liquidity-adjusted sizing
assessment.self_critique: Could have added a router endpoint exposing adjust_weights_for_liquidity directly. Could have integrated liquidity sizing into the main portfolio_engine.py build flow, not just compare_methods. The liquidity penalty curve (exponent=0.5) is a reasonable default but would benefit from backtesting against actual execution costs.

next_steps: Integrate liquidity-adjusted sizing into portfolio_engine.py build flow (not just compare_methods)
Add API endpoint for standalone liquidity adjustment
Wire liquidity score into stock analysis page frontend
Backtest liquidity penalty exponent against realized slippage
Add conformal prediction intervals for crash probabilities
